title: Framework gap FGAP-007 — no staleness engine for research blocks
body: Research entries (per the design in analysis/research-blocks-design.md) must carry grounding metadata (version-pinned dependencies, retrieved revisions, dated external refs) and stale_conditions (e.g. 'pi-ai upgrades past 0.63.x'). Staleness must currently be checked by hand. The framework cannot automatically fire stale_conditions against the current state of installed dependencies, git revisions, or external-URL content hashes — and cannot propagate invalidation downstream through 'informs' edges to flag affected decisions as needing re-grounding. Proposed resolution: add a staleness-engine runtime that reads every research entry's stale_conditions and compares them against the current state on a schedule or on-demand; transitions stale entries to status=stale; walks informs edges to emit structured diagnostics for any enacted decision whose supporting research has gone stale. Depends on the research block kind itself existing (research.json + research.schema.json), which is currently specified in analysis/research-blocks-design.md but not yet enacted. Canonical owner: to be added to .project/framework-gaps.json when the research block kind lands. Canonical vocabulary: grounding decay; citation rot; evidence expiry.
resolved_by: Filed as FGAP-007 in framework-gaps.json (this commit). Issue mirror retired; canonical gap carries the staleness-engine work envelope.
